core_mechanism: spontaneous breaking of a continuous non-abelian global symmetry whose universal long-distance behavior is captured by the effective theory of the resulting Goldstone (soft) modes, realized by engineering a microscopic lattice statistical model into the same symmetry class with a tunable explicit symmetry-breaking term and a controllable order-parameter scale
brief_reason: The abstract builds a strongly-coupled lattice model with an SU(2)xSU(2)xU(1) symmetry, breaks the U_A(1) part via a four-fermion term, tunes the order-parameter scale F small, and shows the microscopic model reproduces the universal predictions of the Goldstone-mode effective theory (chiral perturbation theory) in its soft-mode-dominated finite-volume regime.
